findings.title: learn:update-progress writes progress.json without schema validation
findings.snippet:
// We don't have a dedicated progress schema yet ...
atomicWriteJson(progressPath, progress, () => true);
findings.impact: Progress format can drift silently; downstream tools/agents may break unexpectedly.
findings.recommendation: Introduce learn-progress.schema.json and validate progress writes the same way as plan/profile/state.

findings.title: Locking has no stale-lock recovery (2s timeout, no PID liveness check)
findings.snippet: const timeoutMs = 2000; ... if (Date.now() - start > timeoutMs) { ... throw lockError; }
findings.impact: A crashed process can leave a lock file that blocks all learn:* state operations until manual cleanup.
findings.recommendation: Add stale-lock detection (pid not running / lock age TTL) and a safe forced unlock path.

findings.title: learn:update-progress does not validate status input early; schema failure happens later during plan write
findings.snippet: if (!status) fail(...); ... kp.status = status; ... atomicWriteJson(planPath, plan, getPlanValidator());
findings.impact: Agents get later/less-specific errors; partially mutated progress object may be written before plan validation fails (depending on execution order).
findings.recommendation: Validate status against allowed enum before mutating and writing, and write plan+progress in an all-or-nothing transaction.

areas_remaining: Session/plan write APIs to support execute upgrade paths

leads.description: Compare statuses in learn-plan.schema.json vs progress tracking model
